surface_verification.justification: Framework-internal change to two compliance PreToolUse Bash hooks (robust `uv run --no-project` invocation + a fail-open guard) plus a new pure-stdlib helper lib and a docs row. No product web/api/cli/UI/store surface is introduced or changed. The hook IS the surface: verified end-to-end through the real `uv run --no-project` invocation path -- a non-deploy bash call exits 0 (allow), an internal crash (`tool_input` is a string) exits 0 (FAIL-OPEN) and writes a diagnostic to the gitignored runtime hook-error log, and a deploy-with-findings still exits 2 (deliberate soft-block + 'Continue anyway' override context preserved).

degraded: Full per-plugin monorepo suites delegated to CI ci.yml. Local F0 gate ran: shared/tests (3560 passed, 12 skipped, 26 deselected cross_plugin/slow) + shipwright-compliance plugin suite (734 passed) + integration-tests enforcement+failopen (15 passed) + pinned-ruff clean on all changed files.

behaviors.behavior: run_failopen returns 0 and logs a diagnostic when main() raises (crash never hard-blocks)
run_failopen passes 0 and the deliberate soft-block 2 through unchanged; non-int -> 0
log_hook_error writes to gitignored runtime/hook_errors.log, appends, never raises on a bad root
check_security_scan fails OPEN (exit 0 + diagnostic) on an internal crash, end-to-end as a subprocess
check_rtm_coverage fails OPEN (exit 0 + diagnostic) on an internal crash, end-to-end as a subprocess
compliance hooks.json: both Bash PreToolUse gates carry `--no-project`; file is valid JSON (io-boundary round-trip)
Deliberate deploy-with-findings still soft-blocks (exit 2); RTM-coverage commit gate still soft-blocks (regression preserved)
behaviors.evidence: test_enforcement_hooks.py::TestCheckSecurityScan/TestCheckRtmCoverage + integration test_compliance_enforcement.py (rc==2 cases) PASSED + real `uv run --no-project` smoke exit 2

behaviors.behavior: Override/remediation path preserved: blocked output carries 'Continue anyway' additionalContext -> compliance_overrides.log
